body:
This PR adds a unit test that reproduces the bug found in the FD shmem implementation, and applies the bugfix.

The bug was due to us grabbing the nodal points using `(Nq, _, _, Nv, Nh) = DataLayouts.universal_size(us)`, and this is wrong for 1D spectral element cases, which is exactly the subset of jobs that were failing.

This was a bit tricky to chase down because most kernels were unaffected, but kernels of the form `@. x += div(f)` broke for 1D SEM cases with sufficiently high horizontal resolution, because there is a race condition on reading and writing `x` between multiple threadblocks.
title: Add FD shmem bug reproducer

body:
I tried using the DebugOnly module for an example in ClimaAtmos, and I found NaNs when evaluating `A₂₂′ = @. A₂₂ - A₂₁ * inv(A₁₁) * A₁₂` because the RHS is not yet initialized (this function's responsibility is to allocate cache variables).

This is another similar fix to #2253.

Originally, I tried using `A₂₂′ = similar(A₂₂)`, but the right-hand-side could be promoted from `BidiagonalMatrixRow` eltype to `QuaddiagonalMatrixRow` eltype, so we need to include the whole expression. Rather than writing this as `A₂₂′ = similar(Base.Broadcast.broadcasted(-, A₂₂, Base.Broadcast.broadcasted(*
A₂₁, Base.Broadcast.broadcasted(*, inv(A₁₁), A₁₂))))`, which should work, I thought that it would be cleaner to simply add `LazyBroadcast` as a dependency and write this as: `A₂₂′ = similar(@. lazy(A₂₂ - A₂₁ * inv(A₁₁) * A₁₂))`.
title: Dont materialize bc expr with uninitialized data

body: Makes the "Vectors and Matrices of Fields" API display correctly.
title: Fix missing char in matrix_fields docs
